metadata_information.title: When is growth pro-poor Evidence from the diverse experiences of India's states
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: When is growth pro-poor Evidence from the diverse experiences of India's states

document_description.abstract: The authors use 20 household surveys for India's 15 major states, spanning 1960-94, to study how initial conditions and the sectoral composition of economic growth interact to influence how much economic growth reduced poverty. The elasticities of measured poverty to farm yields and development spending did not differ significantly across states. But the elasticities of poverty to (urban and rural) non-farm output varied appreciably, and the differences were quantitatively important to the overall rate of poverty reduction. States with initially lower farm productivity, lower rural living standards relative to those in urban areas, and lower literacy experienced a less pro-poor growth process.
